Cache Driver Interface

Every custom cache driver must implement this interface. All built-in drivers follow this contract.

options

Section titled ŌĆ£optionsŌĆØ

Driver configuration object.

name

Section titled ŌĆ£nameŌĆØ

Unique driver identifier.

Example:

set(key, value, ttlOrOptions?)

Section titled ŌĆ£set(key, value, ttlOrOptions?)ŌĆØ

Store a value in cache. The third argument accepts three shapes ŌĆö a positional TTL (number or duration string) for the common case, or a rich CacheSetOptions object.

Examples:

See Set Options for the full option reference.

get(key)

Section titled ŌĆ£get(key)ŌĆØ

Retrieve a value from cache. Returns the value if found and not expired, otherwise null.

Example:

has(key)

Section titled ŌĆ£has(key)ŌĆØ

Check if a key exists in cache without retrieving its value. Returns true if key exists and is not expired, false otherwise.

Example:

remove(key)

Section titled ŌĆ£remove(key)ŌĆØ

Delete a single key from cache.

Example:

flush()

Section titled ŌĆ£flush()ŌĆØ

Clear all cached data.

Example:

removeNamespace(namespace)

Section titled ŌĆ£removeNamespace(namespace)ŌĆØ

Delete all keys within a namespace. Namespace is the first segment of a key before the first dot.

Example:

remember(key, ttl, callback)

Section titled ŌĆ£remember(key, ttl, callback)ŌĆØ

Get a value from cache. If not found or expired, execute the callback function and cache the result. Includes automatic stampede prevention within a single process.

Example:

See Stampede Prevention for details.

pull(key)

Section titled ŌĆ£pull(key)ŌĆØ

Retrieve and delete a key atomically.

Example:

forever(key, value)

Section titled ŌĆ£forever(key, value)ŌĆØ

Store a value permanently with no expiration.

Example:

increment(key, value?)

Section titled ŌĆ£increment(key, value?)ŌĆØ

Atomically increase a numeric value by the given amount (default 1). Returns the new value. Throws if the stored value is not numeric.

Example:

decrement(key, value?)

Section titled ŌĆ£decrement(key, value?)ŌĆØ

Atomically decrease a numeric value by the given amount (default 1). Returns the new value. Throws if the stored value is not numeric.

Example:

update(key, fn, options?)

Section titled ŌĆ£update(key, fn, options?)ŌĆØ

Atomically read, transform, and write a cached value. The callback receives the current value (or null on miss) and returns the next value. Returning null removes the entry.

Example:

Throws CacheUnsupportedError on the file driver. See Update & Merge.

merge(key, partial, options?)

Section titled ŌĆ£merge(key, partial, options?)ŌĆØ

Shallow-merge a partial object into a cached value. Treats missing keys as {}.

Example:

Throws CacheUnsupportedError on the file driver.

list(key)

Section titled ŌĆ£list(key)ŌĆØ

Obtain a list accessor bound to the key. Returns a CacheListAccessor<T> for queue/stack/recent-N workflows.

Example:

See Cache Lists for the full accessor API.

many(keys)

Section titled ŌĆ£many(keys)ŌĆØ

Retrieve multiple values in one operation. Returns an array in the same order as the input keys. Missing or expired keys return null in their positions.

Example:

setMany(items, ttl?)

Section titled ŌĆ£setMany(items, ttl?)ŌĆØ

Store multiple key-value pairs in one operation.

Example:

tags(tags)

Section titled ŌĆ£tags(tags)ŌĆØ

Create a tagged cache instance to group related keys. Returns a TaggedCacheDriver instance.

Example:

similar(vector, options)

Section titled ŌĆ£similar(vector, options)ŌĆØ

Vector-based retrieval. Returns the nearest stored entries to vector by cosine similarity, ordered by descending score.

Example:

Drivers that donŌĆÖt index vectors throw CacheUnsupportedError. See Similarity Retrieval for the full API and capability matrix.

parseKey(key)

Section titled ŌĆ£parseKey(key)ŌĆØ

Normalize a cache key for driver storage. Sanitizes special characters and applies global prefix.

Key parsing rules:

  • String keys: strip {}"[], replace : and , with .
  • Object keys: JSON stringify then apply string rules
  • Global prefix is prepended if configured

Example:

setOptions(options)

Section titled ŌĆ£setOptions(options)ŌĆØ

Update driver configuration at runtime.

connect()

Section titled ŌĆ£connect()ŌĆØ

Establish connection to the cache backend.

disconnect()

Section titled ŌĆ£disconnect()ŌĆØ

Close connection to the cache backend.

client

Section titled ŌĆ£clientŌĆØ

Optional reference to the underlying backend client (Redis connection, Memcached instance, etc.).

on / off / once

Section titled ŌĆ£on / off / onceŌĆØ

Event subscription methods. Return the driver instance for chaining.

See Event System for the full event catalog.

setLoggingState(shouldLog)

Section titled ŌĆ£setLoggingState(shouldLog)ŌĆØ

Toggle per-driver operation logging at runtime.

Complete Interface Definition

Section titled ŌĆ£Complete Interface DefinitionŌĆØ

Return types

Section titled ŌĆ£Return typesŌĆØ

CacheSetResult

Section titled ŌĆ£CacheSetResultŌĆØ

Returned by conditional writes (onConflict: "create" or "update"):

See Set Options ŌĆö Conditional writes.

CacheSimilarHit / CacheSimilarOptions

Section titled ŌĆ£CacheSimilarHit / CacheSimilarOptionsŌĆØ

Used by similar():

See Similarity Retrieval.

CacheListAccessor

Section titled ŌĆ£CacheListAccessorŌĆØ

Returned by list():

See Cache Lists.

Error types

Section titled ŌĆ£Error typesŌĆØ

Drivers throw specific error classes ŌĆö consumers should catch these selectively:

  • CacheConfigurationError ŌĆö bad options, invalid TTL string, conflicting ttl + expiresAt
  • CacheDriverNotInitializedError ŌĆö operations called before init()
  • CacheUnsupportedError ŌĆö driver doesnŌĆÖt implement the requested op (today: update / merge on file)
  • CacheConcurrencyError ŌĆö reserved for future WATCH/MULTI retry exhaustion
  • CacheConnectionError ŌĆö reserved for driver connection failures

See Errors for the full catalog.

Event Types

Section titled ŌĆ£Event TypesŌĆØ

The event system emits:

  • hit ŌĆö cache hit (value retrieved)
  • miss ŌĆö cache miss (value not found or expired)
  • set ŌĆö value stored in cache
  • removed ŌĆö key removed from cache
  • flushed ŌĆö entire cache cleared
  • expired ŌĆö key expired (TTL reached)
  • connected ŌĆö driver connected
  • disconnected ŌĆö driver disconnected
  • error ŌĆö error occurred during operation

See Event System.

Implementing a Custom Driver

Section titled ŌĆ£Implementing a Custom DriverŌĆØ
  • Extend BaseCacheDriver to reuse key parsing, logging, TTL handling, event emission, and default implementations for has, remember, pull, forever, increment, decrement, many, setMany, tags, update, merge, and list.
  • Implement these required abstract methods: set(), get(), remove(), flush(), removeNamespace().
  • In set(), call this.resolveSetOptions(ttlOrOptions) to normalize the third argument into { ttl, tags, onConflict }.
  • Override update / merge / list only when your driver can offer stronger semantics (e.g. Redis-native commands). Throw CacheUnsupportedError from any op your driver canŌĆÖt safely support.

Example skeleton

Section titled ŌĆ£Example skeletonŌĆØ

See Make Your Own Cache Driver for a complete end-to-end example.
